result.markdown:
# Plot Options {#sec-plots}

The `plot.sc_fit()` method ships two plot types. Both return plain
`ggplot` objects, so users can layer additional geoms, facets, or themes
on top.

## Beta ridgelines

The default `"beta_ridgelines"` draws one density ridgeline per
attribute dummy over the held-out per-respondent
$\hat\beta_j(\mathbf Z_i)$. It is the most compact visual summary of
the direction-versus-intensity decomposition: a ridgeline centered away
from zero indicates directional consensus; a wide ridgeline indicates
high intensity heterogeneity.




::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(fit_sw, "beta_ridgelines")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](06-plot-options_files/figure-html/plot-ridgelines-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


The ridgeline layer is drawn by `ggridges::geom_density_ridges` when
`ggridges` is available; a boxplot fallback kicks in otherwise so the
plot method works in minimal installations.

## Loss trace

`"loss_trace"` plots the per-fold training loss curves. The curves
should visually level off before the last epoch; if they are still
descending, increase `n_epochs`.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(fit_sw, "loss_trace")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](06-plot-options_files/figure-html/plot-loss-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## Customization with ggplot2

Since both plot types return `ggplot` objects, standard `ggplot2`
modifications apply directly.


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ggplot2)
plot(fit_sw, "beta_ridgelines") +
  labs(title = "Per-respondent preference distributions",
       subtitle = "Saha-Weeks candidate choice") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](06-plot-options_files/figure-html/plot-custom-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::



::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
plot(fit_sw, "loss_trace") +
  labs(title = "Training convergence") +
  theme_bw()
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](06-plot-options_files/figure-html/plot-loss-custom-1.png){width=672}
:::
:::
